query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Deceive', 'Cause to believe something false', 'The author hoped the new policy would deceive tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),

  ('Decency', 'Conformity to accepted standards of respectability', 'Over time, the community learned to decency its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deciduous', 'Falling off or shed at a particular season, point of growth', 'What seemed deciduous at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Decimal', 'Pertaining to or proceeding by tenths or the power of ten', 'The author hoped the new policy would decimal tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),

  ('Decipher', 'To succeed in understanding or interpreting', 'Over time, the community learned to decipher its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Decisive', 'Determining an outcome or showing firmness', '“Her decisive leadership helped the team succeed.”', 'adjective', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Declamation', 'Rhetorical delivery, loud speaking in public', 'The essay highlights the declamation of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Declamatory', 'Bombastic, ostentatiously lofty in style', 'Scholars noted a striking declamatory between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Declarative', 'A statement in the form of a formal announcement', 'The essay highlights the declarative of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Declension', 'The inflection of a part of speech, such as case and number', 'The essay highlights the declension of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Decorate', 'To furnish with ornaments', 'Over time, the community learned to decorate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Decorous', 'Keeping with good taste and propriety', 'The reviewer described the tone as decorous, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Decorum', 'Behavior in keeping with good taste', 'Scholars noted a striking decorum between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Decoy', 'A person who entices or lures another person or thing', 'Her decoy became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Decrepit', 'Weakened or worn out from age or wear', 'Scholars noted a striking decrepit between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Decry', 'To publicly denounce', 'The author hoped the new policy would decry tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dedication', 'The quality of being committed to a task or purpose', 'Over time, the community learned to dedication its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deduce', 'To reach a conclusion through reasoning', '“From the evidence, detectives deduced the suspect’s identity.”', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deface', 'To damage something in a visible or conspicuous manner', 'The author hoped the new policy would deface tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Defalcate', 'To misappropriate funds, to embezzle', 'The author hoped the new policy would defalcate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Defamation', 'Act of injuring another''s reputation by slander or libel', 'The essay highlights the defamation of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Defamatory', 'Damaging to someone''s reputation, especially if untrue', 'Critics argued that the plan might defamatory the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Defame', 'To try to diminish the reputation of', 'Critics argued that the plan might defame the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Defendant', 'A person or group against whom a charge is brought in court', 'Scholars noted a striking defendant between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Defensible', 'Capable of being defended against armed attack', 'Scholars noted a striking defensible between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Defensive', 'Used or intended to defend or protect', 'Critics argued that the plan might defensive the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Defer', 'To postpone or delay', '“They decided to defer the meeting until next week.”', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deference', 'Humble submission and respect', 'Scholars noted a striking deference between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deferential', 'Respectful, reverential', 'Her deferential became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Defiance', 'Open resistance, bold disobedience', 'The passage presents a defiance portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Defiant', 'Showing open resistance, provocative', 'The passage presents a defiant portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deficiency', 'Inadequacy or incompleteness', 'Scholars noted a striking deficiency between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deficient', 'Lacking something essential, often construed with in', 'The passage presents a deficient portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Defile', 'To make impure, to make dirty', 'The author hoped the new policy would defile tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Define', 'To explain or describe the precise meaning of something', 'Critics argued that the plan might define the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Definite', 'Having distinct limits', 'The reviewer described the tone as definite, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Definitive', 'Explicitly defined', 'The passage presents a definitive portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deflect', 'To make (something) deviate from its original path', 'Over time, the community learned to deflect its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Defoliate', 'To remove foliage from one or more plants', 'Critics argued that the plan might defoliate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deforest', 'To destroy or to fell all the trees of a forest', 'Critics argued that the plan might deforest the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deform', 'To remove the form of', 'Critics argued that the plan might deform the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deformity', 'A misshapen part, esp.  of the body', 'Scholars noted a striking deformity between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Defraud', 'To obtain money or property by fraud, to swindle', 'The author hoped the new policy would defraud tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Defray', 'To spend (money)', 'Over time, the community learned to defray its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deft', 'Quick and neat in action, skillful', 'Scholars noted a striking deft between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Defunct', 'No longer existing or functioning', 'The essay highlights the defunct of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Defuse', 'To reduce the danger or tension in (a difficult situation)', 'Over time, the community learned to defuse its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Defy', 'To openly resist or refuse to obey', 'Critics argued that the plan might defy the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
